name: Sauteed Rabbit Loin with Braised Fennel and Balsamic Vinegar
recipeIngredient: 4 boneless rabbit loins with flap intact
1 large bulb fennel (about 1 pound) core removed and sliced into 1/4-inch batonette
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse and pat dry rabbit loins.
2. In a mixing bowl, stir together cold water, vinegar, salt, and peppercorns.
3. Place rabbit loins in liquid and allow to stand 1 hour. Remove rabbit from brine and pat dry.
4. Heat 4 tablespoons olive oil in a 12- to 14-inch heavy bottomed sauté pan until smoking.
5. Season rabbit pieces with salt and pepper, then sauté until golden brown on both sides, about 6 to 7 minutes. Remove rabbit pieces and set aside.
6. Add fennel, onion, and fennel seeds to the pan and cook until soft and lightly browned, about 10 minutes.
7. Add tomato sauce, wine, and balsamic vinegar, then bring to a boil.
8. Place rabbit pieces back in the pan and simmer for 15 minutes uncovered.
9. Meanwhile, make salsa verde:
- Soak bread in white wine vinegar for about 2 minutes.
- Remove bread from vinegar and squeeze dry.
- In a food processor, blend parsley, capers, garlic, spinach, thyme, fennel fronds, and extra virgin olive oil until smooth. Set aside.
10. Remove cooked rabbit pieces from the sauce and arrange on a serving platter.
11. Top with fennel sauce mixture, drizzle with salsa verde, and serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings
